---
title: "Theorem 7.1 ‚Äî Consistency of OLS"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.olsBetaOrZero_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta"
chapter: 7
---

## Textbook statement

Suppose we observe an i.i.d. sample $(X_i, e_i)$ drawn from the population
$(X, e)$ on a probability space $(\Omega, \mu)$, and form the response
$y_i = X_i^\top \beta + e_i$. Stack the first $n$ observations into a regressor
matrix $X_{(n)}$ and outcome vector $y_{(n)}$. Under the moment assumptions
that $\mathbb{E}[X_i X_i^\top]$ is invertible, $\mathbb{E}\|X_i\|^2 < \infty$,
and $\mathbb{E}\|X_i e_i\| < \infty$, the OLS estimator from the stacked
sample converges in probability to $\beta$:

$$
\hat\beta_{(n)} \xrightarrow{p} \beta \qquad \text{as } n \to \infty.
$$

This is "consistency": OLS recovers the true parameter $\beta$ in the
large-sample limit.

## Assumptions

Hansen Theorem 7.1 requires:

- **Linear model with stable parameter:** $y_i = X_i^\top \beta + e_i$ for all
  $i$, with $\beta \in \mathbb{R}^k$ fixed.
- **i.i.d. sampling** under measure $\mu$.
- **Population Gram matrix invertibility:** $\mathbb{E}[X_i X_i^\top] = Q$ is
  invertible (full-rank regressors in population).
- **Finite second moment of regressors:** $\mathbb{E}\|X_i\|^2 < \infty$ ‚Äî
  needed for the WLLN of the sample Gram.
- **Finite first moment of cross-product:** $\mathbb{E}\|X_i e_i\| < \infty$ ‚Äî
  needed for the WLLN of the sample cross-moment.

In Lean, these are bundled into a single typeclass
`SampleMomentAssumption71 Œº X e`.

## Lean statement

The textbook-facing theorem lives in
`HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean`:

- [`olsBetaOrZero_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1183)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:1183`.

```lean
theorem olsBetaOrZero_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta
    {Œº : Measure Œ©} [IsFiniteMeasure Œº]
    {X : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí (k ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} {e : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {y : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (Œ≤ : k ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
    (h : SampleMomentAssumption71 Œº X e)
    (hmodel : ‚àÄ i œâ, y i œâ = (X i œâ) ‚¨ù·µ• Œ≤ + e i œâ) :
    TendstoInMeasure Œº
      (fun n œâ => olsBetaOrZero (stackRegressors X n œâ) (stackOutcomes y n œâ))
      atTop (fun _ => Œ≤)
```

### Reading the statement

In English: **given** a probability space $(\Omega, \mu)$, an i.i.d. sequence
of regressor rows $X_i$ and errors $e_i$ satisfying the moment assumptions,
and a true coefficient $\beta$ that links outcomes to regressors via
$y_i = X_i^\top \beta + e_i$, the OLS estimator built from the first $n$
stacked observations converges in probability to $\beta$ as $n \to \infty$.

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `{Œº : Measure Œ©}` | $\mu$ on $\Omega$ | **Given:** the underlying measure on the sample space. Implicit (`{...}`), inferred from later arguments. |
| `[IsFiniteMeasure Œº]` | $\mu(\Omega) < \infty$ | **Given (hypothesis):** finite total mass ‚Äî so probability statements like `TendstoInMeasure` are well-typed. Square brackets mark this as a typeclass instance. |
| `{X : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí (k ‚Üí ‚Ñù)}` | $X_i : \Omega \to \mathbb{R}^k$ | **Given:** the i.i.d. sequence of regressor row processes ‚Äî observation $i$ at sample point $\omega$ is the row vector $X_i(\omega)$. |
| `{e : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù}` | $e_i : \Omega \to \mathbb{R}$ | **Given:** the i.i.d. sequence of scalar error processes. |
| `{y : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù}` | $y_i : \Omega \to \mathbb{R}$ | **Given:** the i.i.d. sequence of scalar outcomes. Logically determined by `X`, `e`, and `Œ≤` via `hmodel`. |
| `(Œ≤ : k ‚Üí ‚Ñù)` | $\beta \in \mathbb{R}^k$ | **Given:** the true (population) coefficient vector ‚Äî a fixed parameter, not random. |
| `(h : SampleMomentAssumption71 Œº X e)` | i.i.d. + moment + invertibility bundle | **Given (hypothesis):** the bundled Theorem 7.1 assumptions: i.i.d. structure, integrability of $X_i$, $X_i X_i^\top$ and $X_i e_i$, plus invertibility of the population Gram $\mathbb{E}[X_i X_i^\top]$. Each face is exposed as a separate field on `h`. |
| `(hmodel : ‚àÄ i œâ, y i œâ = (X i œâ) ‚¨ù·µ• Œ≤ + e i œâ)` | $y_i = X_i^\top \beta + e_i$ | **Given (hypothesis):** the linear model holds **pointwise** at every observation $i$ and every sample point $\omega$. `‚¨ù·µ•` is `Matrix.dotProduct` (vector inner product). |
| `TendstoInMeasure Œº ‚Ä¶ atTop (fun _ => Œ≤)` | $\hat\beta_n \xrightarrow{p} \beta$ | **Conclusion:** convergence in probability under $\mu$ as $n \to \infty$. The constant target `fun _ => Œ≤` is the fixed limit. |
| `fun n œâ => olsBetaOrZero (stackRegressors X n œâ) (stackOutcomes y n œâ)` | $\hat\beta_{(n)}(\omega)$ | The sample OLS estimator: `stackRegressors X n œâ` builds the $n \times k$ design from the first $n$ rows at $\omega$, `stackOutcomes y n œâ` stacks the first $n$ outcomes, and `olsBetaOrZero` returns the closed-form OLS coefficient (or $0$ on the singular set). |

(The ambient typeclasses `[Fintype k] [DecidableEq k]` for the regressor index
type, and the universe `Œ© : Type*`, are hoisted into a `variable` block at the
top of the file's section.)

The Lean development uses a **totalized** estimator
`olsBetaStar` (defined for every design and returning $0$ when $X^\top X$ is
singular) together with a wrapper `olsBetaOrZero` that agrees with ordinary
`olsBeta` whenever the sample Gram is nonsingular and equals `olsBetaStar`
unconditionally. The asymptotic statement uses `olsBetaOrZero` because
measurability is cleaner ‚Äî singular samples have measure zero in the limit, so
the wrapper's behavior on those samples doesn't matter.

The substantive work is done by the totalized companion theorem:

- [`olsBetaStar_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L960)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:960`.

The wrapper version reduces to it via `olsBetaOrZero_eq_olsBetaStar`.

## Translation notes

- Hansen writes $\hat\beta_n \to_p \beta$. Lean writes
  `TendstoInMeasure Œº (fun n œâ => olsBetaOrZero ‚Ä¶) atTop (fun _ => Œ≤)`. These
  are the same notion: convergence in probability under measure $\mu$.
- The `SampleMomentAssumption71` typeclass packages: `IsFiniteMeasure Œº`,
  i.i.d. structure, integrability of $X_i$, integrability of
  $X_i \cdot X_i$ and $X_i e_i$, plus invertibility of
  $\mathbb{E}[X X^\top]$. Each face is exposed as a separate lemma so that
  downstream proofs can pick out exactly the moment hypothesis they need.
- The Lean form uses `stackRegressors X n` and `stackOutcomes y n` to build
  the sample design matrix and outcome vector from the i.i.d. processes
  `X : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí (k ‚Üí ‚Ñù)` and `y : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù`. This exposes the stacking as a
  measurable function rather than a hand-waved "first $n$ observations."
- The theorem is stated for `olsBetaOrZero`, not `olsBeta`, because the
  latter requires `[Invertible (X·µÄ * X)]` as a typeclass ‚Äî which we cannot
  guarantee on every $\omega$. `olsBetaOrZero` returns $0$ outside the
  nonsingular set; on the asymptotic limit set, it agrees with ordinary OLS.

## Proof sketch

The standard textbook argument:

1. **Sample-moment WLLN.** By the i.i.d. WLLN applied to $X_i X_i^\top$ and
   $X_i e_i$:
   $$
   \hat Q_n := \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n X_i X_i^\top \xrightarrow{p} Q,
   \qquad
   \hat g_n := \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n X_i e_i \xrightarrow{p} \mathbb{E}[X_i e_i].
   $$
2. **Cross moment is zero in the linear model.** Under the linear-model
   hypothesis the cross-moment limit reduces to $0$ (the Lean development
   states this directly as a tendsto-to-zero claim, sidestepping the
   intermediate orthogonality identity).
3. **Continuous mapping.** Since $Q$ is invertible and inversion is continuous
   on the open set of invertible matrices, $\hat Q_n^{-1} \xrightarrow{p}
   Q^{-1}$ on the limit set.
4. **OLS error decomposition.** $\hat\beta_n - \beta = \hat Q_n^{-1} \hat
   g_n$, so by Slutsky's theorem
   $$
   \hat\beta_n - \beta \xrightarrow{p} Q^{-1} \cdot 0 = 0.
   $$

## Lean proof structure

The Lean proof breaks into many supporting lemmas; the headline theorem is
short because it composes pieces.

The deterministic algebraic engine:

- [`olsBeta_sub_eq_sampleGram_inv_mulVec_sampleCrossMoment`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Basic.lean#L303)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Basic.lean:303` ‚Äî the pointwise
  identity $\hat\beta - \beta = \hat Q_n^{-1}\, \hat g_n$ on nonsingular
  samples.

The sample-moment WLLN faces:

- [`sampleGram_stackRegressors_tendstoInMeasure_popGram`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L189)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:189` ‚Äî $\hat Q_n
  \xrightarrow{p} Q$.
- [`sampleGramInv_stackRegressors_tendstoInMeasure_popGramInv`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L244)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:244` ‚Äî $\hat Q_n^{-1}
  \xrightarrow{p} Q^{-1}$ via continuous mapping.
- [`sampleCrossMoment_stackRegressors_stackErrors_tendstoInMeasure_zero`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L261)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:261` ‚Äî $\hat g_n
  \xrightarrow{p} 0$ in the linear-model setup.

The Slutsky / continuous-mapping bridge that combines them:

- [`sampleGramInv_mulVec_sampleCrossMoment_e_tendstoInMeasure_zero`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L411)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:411` ‚Äî combines $\hat
  Q_n^{-1} \to_p Q^{-1}$ with $\hat g_n \to_p 0$ to conclude $\hat
  Q_n^{-1}\, \hat g_n \to_p 0$.

The totalized consistency theorem packages all of this:

- [`olsBetaStar_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L960)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:960`.

The ordinary-wrapper version on line 1822 is then a one-liner:

```lean
simpa [olsBetaOrZero_eq_olsBetaStar] using
  olsBetaStar_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta
    (Œº := Œº) (X := X) (e := e) (y := y) Œ≤ h hmodel
```

### Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

**The headline wrapper (line 1822).** The full proof body of
`olsBetaOrZero_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta` is two lines:

```lean
theorem olsBetaOrZero_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta ‚Ä¶ := by
  simpa [olsBetaOrZero_eq_olsBetaStar] using
    olsBetaStar_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta
      (Œº := Œº) (X := X) (e := e) (y := y) Œ≤ h hmodel
```

There is essentially nothing to walk through here ‚Äî the wrapper version
reduces immediately to the totalized version. The
[`olsBetaOrZero_eq_olsBetaStar`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Basic.lean#L56)
rewrite (line 265) replaces `olsBetaOrZero` by `olsBetaStar` pointwise, and
`simpa` applies that rewrite to both sides of the totalized statement to
produce the wrapper statement verbatim.

**The totalized version (line 1600).** The substantive proof is
[`olsBetaStar_stack_tendstoInMeasure_beta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L960).
It is too long to walk line by line; instead, here is the role each composed
lemma plays in the high-level structure (the proof is built around the
identity $\hat\beta_n - \beta = R'_n + \hat Q_n^{-1}\,\hat g_n$, where $R'_n$
is a "residual" term that vanishes on the nonsingular event):

- **Pointwise residual identity** ‚Äî
  [`olsBetaStar_sub_identity`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Basic.lean#L505)
  (line 714) gives, for **every** $\omega$, the algebraic decomposition
  $\hat\beta_n - \beta - \hat Q_n^{-1}\,\hat g_n = (\hat Q_n^{-1}\hat Q_n - 1)\,\beta$.
  The right-hand side is exactly $R'_n$ ‚Äî it equals $0$ on $\{\hat Q_n
  \text{ invertible}\}$ and $-\beta$ off it.
- **Algebraic engine on nonsingular samples** ‚Äî
  [`olsBeta_sub_eq_sampleGram_inv_mulVec_sampleCrossMoment`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Basic.lean#L303)
  (line 512) is the deterministic Hansen ¬ß7.2 identity $\hat\beta_n - \beta =
  \hat Q_n^{-1}\,\hat g_n$ on the invertible event. `olsBetaStar_sub_identity`
  is its `olsBetaStar`-on-all-of-$\Omega$ extension.
- **Residual vanishes in probability** ‚Äî
  [`residual_tendstoInMeasure_zero`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L504)
  (line 1144) shows $R'_n \xrightarrow{p} 0$. Inside, it uses
  `measure_sampleGram_singular_tendsto_zero` (the singular set has
  vanishing measure) plus the fact that $R'_n \equiv 0$ on the nonsingular
  event. This is loaded as `hF6` and immediately re-shaped via the residual
  identity to give the "$R'_n$" tendsto piece.
- **Sample Gram WLLN** ‚Äî
  [`sampleGram_stackRegressors_tendstoInMeasure_popGram`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L189)
  (line 838) gives $\hat Q_n \xrightarrow{p} Q$ via the i.i.d. WLLN applied to
  $X_i X_i^\top$ (uses the second-moment hypothesis from `h`).
- **Inversion-continuity** ‚Äî
  [`sampleGramInv_stackRegressors_tendstoInMeasure_popGramInv`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L244)
  (line 875) lifts the Gram WLLN through matrix inversion: since matrix
  inversion is continuous at any nonsingular point and `h.Q_nonsing`
  guarantees $Q$ is invertible, $\hat Q_n^{-1} \xrightarrow{p} Q^{-1}$ on the
  limit set.
- **Cross-moment WLLN** ‚Äî
  [`sampleCrossMoment_stackRegressors_stackErrors_tendstoInMeasure_zero`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L261)
  (line 892) gives $\hat g_n \xrightarrow{p} 0$. The cross-product
  $X_i \cdot e_i$ has mean $0$ in the linear-model setup (Hansen's
  orthogonality axiom) and finite first moment, so the WLLN delivers a zero
  limit.
- **Slutsky combination** ‚Äî
  [`sampleGramInv_mulVec_sampleCrossMoment_e_tendstoInMeasure_zero`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L411)
  (line 1042) combines the inverted-Gram tendsto with the cross-moment
  tendsto via `tendstoInMeasure_mulVec` to conclude $\hat Q_n^{-1}\,\hat g_n
  \xrightarrow{p} Q^{-1} \cdot 0 = 0$. This is loaded as `hCore`.
- **Add the two convergences** ‚Äî the proof then uses
  `tendstoInMeasure_add` to combine $R'_n \xrightarrow{p} 0$ (`hR'`) and
  $\hat Q_n^{-1}\,\hat g_n \xrightarrow{p} 0$ (`hCore`), giving
  $R'_n + \hat Q_n^{-1}\,\hat g_n \xrightarrow{p} 0$. A second
  `tendstoInMeasure_add` against the constant tendsto for $\beta$ shifts the
  limit to $\beta$.
- **Congr back to `olsBetaStar`** ‚Äî finally, `hSumPlus.congr_left` rewrites
  the sample function $R'_n + \hat Q_n^{-1}\hat g_n + \beta$ back to
  $\hat\beta_n^*$ using `olsBetaStar_sub_identity` once more, closing the
  goal.

(The intervening `hGram_meas`, `hCrossE_meas`, `hInv_meas`, `hCoreMV_meas`,
`hR'_meas` blocks at lines 1608-1656 are all measurability boilerplate ‚Äî
they expand each sample function into a `(1/n) ‚Ä¢ ‚àë ‚Ä¶` form and chain
`AEStronglyMeasurable` through the algebra. They feed the `tendstoInMeasure_add`
and `tendstoInMeasure_mulVec` calls but contribute nothing mathematically
beyond "everything in sight is measurable.")

## Downstream uses

- **Theorem 7.8** (continuous mapping for OLS): if $\phi$ is continuous at
  $\beta$, then $\phi(\hat\beta_n) \xrightarrow{p} \phi(\beta)$. Lean:
  [`continuousAt_function_olsBetaOrZero_tendstoInMeasure`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1214)
  at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean:1214`.
- **Theorem 7.4** (variance estimator consistency): builds on $\hat\beta
  \to_p \beta$ to show $\hat\sigma^2 \to_p \sigma^2$.
- **Theorem 7.3** (asymptotic normality): consistency is a precondition for
  the CLT-style result.
